chunks.text: C.P. No.D-1064 of 2022 & others 1 | P a g e ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petitions No. D – 1064, 1068, 1069, 1072, 1075, 1077, 1080, 1081, 1083, 1084, 1087, 1088, 1089, 1091, 1096, 1097, 1098, 1099, 1103, 1104, 1105, 1106, 1108, 1112, 1114, 1125, 1126, 1127, 1132, 1135, 1141, 1146, 1147, 1148, 1151, 1152, 1153, 1154, 1155, 1160, 1162, 1169, 1174, 1180, 1191, 1193, 1213, 1217, 1235, 1259, 1277, 1287, 1300, 1468, 1549, 1599 of 2022& 02/2023 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Mr. Justice Salahuddin Panhwar Mr. Justice Abdul Mobeen Lakho For hearing of main cases: 25.01.2023 M/s Ghulam Shabbir Shar, Fidaullah Qureshi, Muhammad Raza Soomro, Sohail Ahmed Khoso, Abdul Qadeer Khoso, Waqar Ali Phulpoto, Muhammad Qasim Kandhro, Ubedullah Malano, Riaz Ali Shaikh, Athar Hussain Abro, Niazuddin N. Memon, Nusrat Hussain Memon, Shabbir Ali Bozdar, Badaruddin Memon, Muhammad Qayyum Arain, Deedar Ali M. Chohan, Abdul Mujeeb Shaikh and Achar Khan Gabol, Humail Rafi, Abdul Ghani, Sadaqat Ali Rid & Samiullah Rind, Advocates for the petitioners in C. Ps. No. D-1064, 1072, 1080, 1081, 1083, 1098, 1104, 1125, 1126, 1132, 1148, 1151, 1169, 1174, 1180, 1191, 1193, 1217, 1235, 1287 and 1549 of 2022. M/s Jhando Khan Jarwar, Waqar Ali Phulpoto, Gul Feroze Kalwar, Asif Ali Bozdar, Muhammad Balam Sangi, Riaz Ali Maitlo, Aijaz Ali Shar, Samiullah Rind and Shabbir Hussain Talpur, petitioners in person in C. Ps. No. D-1084, 1088, 1091, 1096, 1099, 1135, 1147, 1152 and 1153 of 2022. Mr. Sanwan Khan Jagirani, Legal Advisor of SIDA along with Faisal Ahmed (Executive Engineer, Nara Canal Division, Khairpur @ Sukkur).

Mr. Sanwan Khan Jagirani, Legal Advisor of SIDA along with Faisal Ahmed (Executive Engineer, Nara Canal Division, Khairpur @ Sukkur). Mr. Nisar Ahmed G. Abro, Deputy Attorney General and Mr. Muhammad Aslam Jatoi, Assistant Attorney General along with Abdul Karim Memon (Chief Operating Officer, SEPCO, Sukkur) and Altaf Hussain Kaheri (Executive Engineer/Operations Division, SEPCO, Ranipur). Mr. Shafi Muhammad Chandio, Additional Advocate General Sindh and M/s Mehboob Ali Wassan and Asfandyar Kharal, Assistant Advocate Generals Sindh along with, Sharjeel Noor Channa (Deputy Commissioner, Khairpur),Muhammad Usman Abdullah Deputy Commissioner, Ghotki, Maqsood Hussain Soomro, Deputy Director, PDMA Sindh, Liaquat Ali C.P. No.D-1064 of 2022 & others 2 | P a g e Kalhoro ADC-I, Sukkur Waseem Ahmed, AC-Rohri, Muhammad Adeel, Assistant Commissioner, Salehpat, Asadullah Khokhar, Assistant Commissioner, Mehrabpur Sajjad Ali Khaskheli, Mukhtiarkar Taluka Hussain Bux Mari, District Mirpurkhas, Dr. Jameel Ahmed, DHO Sukkur, Dr. Abdul Sattar, Director Health Services, Sukkur, Dr. Rehmatullah Solangi, DHO Khairpur, Rasool Bux Junejo, Abdul Majeed Panhyar, Executive Engineer, Provincial Highway Division Shaheed Benazirabad, Khursheed Ahmed, XEN Mirpur Mathelo, Irrigation Ghotki, Darya Khan Chandio, AC Kotdiji, Dr. Nadeem Memon, Focal Person, DHO Office Naushahro Feroze, Jawed Memon, Project Director, Sindh Flood Emergency Rehabilitation Project, Muhammad Hayat Shaikh (Project Director SCARP, Khairpur), Amjad Ahmed Memon (Project Director SCARP, North Rohri/Shaheed Benazirabad), Zain-ul-Abidin Morio (Executive Engineer, Provincial Highways, Khairpur), Qaisar Ali Hashmi, District Accounts Officer, Sukkur. ----- Salahuddin Panhwar, J.- Pursuant to order dated 12.01.2023, Project Director, Sindh Flood Emergency Rehabilitation Project (SFERP), Irrigation Component, Karachi appeared and submitted the report.

Rehmatullah Solangi, DHO Khairpur, Rasool Bux Junejo, Abdul Majeed Panhyar, Executive Engineer, Provincial Highway Division Shaheed Benazirabad, Khursheed Ahmed, XEN Mirpur Mathelo, Irrigation Ghotki, Darya Khan Chandio, AC Kotdiji, Dr. Nadeem Memon, Focal Person, DHO Office Naushahro Feroze, Jawed Memon, Project Director, Sindh Flood Emergency Rehabilitation Project, Muhammad Hayat Shaikh (Project Director SCARP, Khairpur), Amjad Ahmed Memon (Project Director SCARP, North Rohri/Shaheed Benazirabad), Zain-ul-Abidin Morio (Executive Engineer, Provincial Highways, Khairpur), Qaisar Ali Hashmi, District Accounts Officer, Sukkur. ----- Salahuddin Panhwar, J.- Pursuant to order dated 12.01.2023, Project Director, Sindh Flood Emergency Rehabilitation Project (SFERP), Irrigation Component, Karachi appeared and submitted the report. According to Project Director, (PD) as per commitment with World Bank, Sindh Government will repair and recoup Dohras for rehabilitation of flood affected areas including construction of houses, compensation and re-modeling of NALAs and rehabilitation of all ways of water including lacks as well as natural drainage sources by two different projects. At present, they have received 200 Million Dollars only. Project Director and one Deputy Director, who is executive engineer, are co-signatory to utilize the amount received under this head to recoup and remodeling the capacity of Manchur Lake and other water sources. At this juncture, PD contends that they have utilized 3.8 million rupees on different schemes. Details of schemes are attached, accordingly PD shall submit progress report along with photographs. 2. Besides, in view of the Flood Commission Report (as discussed and referred to in earlier order), SCARP has submitted feasibility report in view of the flood commission in 2013 for activation and construction of Dhoras and other natural water ways. Such summary was approved. Nevertheless, according to PD this task is C.P. No.D-1064 of 2022 & others 3 | P a g e assigned to same project, hence in view of the Flood Commission Report 2013, paragraph No.15 of order dated 12.01.2023, being relevant, is reproduced herewith:- “15. Project Director, SCARP contends that they are unable to drain out the water due to blockage of natural way(s), through illegal allotments of the land and encroachment by influential persons. He also contends that there is no proper drainage Nalla (s) in Khairpur District and adjoining Districts as well, and there is no chance to remove the flood water in near future.

Project Director, SCARP contends that they are unable to drain out the water due to blockage of natural way(s), through illegal allotments of the land and encroachment by influential persons. He also contends that there is no proper drainage Nalla (s) in Khairpur District and adjoining Districts as well, and there is no chance to remove the flood water in near future. This contention of PD depicts his inefficiency and ineffectiveness of the Commission and Authorities. However, he contended that summary submitted by the SCARP with the Worthy Chief Minister has been approved due to objection by P&D, they have submitted concept paper for updating of the feasibility report 2013, resultantly hurdles are removed and now funds are to be released. Being relevant, component of project as approved are that:- The Project comprises of following 08 Components. i. Activation of Dhoro Puran from Mirpur Khas up to Shakoor Dhand. ii. Activation of Sohni Dhoro from Shahdadpur to Mirwah Gorhani. Activation of Bhai Khan Dhoro from Tando Allahyar to Pangrio. iii. Construction of Dhora bye-passes for Digri, Jhuddo, Tando Ghulam Ali and Naukot Towns. iv. Activation Hiral escape/Pithoro Dhoro, Hakro Dhoro & Naro (Nabisar) Dhoro. v. Activation of Sarfaraz (Digri) Dhoro, Pangrio, Khairpur. Khairpur Ghumbo & Roshan abad Dhoro. vi. Activation of Khairpur South dhoras. C.P. No.D-1064 of 2022 & others 4 | P a g e vii. Activation of Ghotki Dhoras. Activation of Miranpur-Bachal Rahu Dhoro, Kandiaro-Moro Dhoro.” 3. Accordingly Flood Commission Report and PC-1 as referred above shall be initiated and implemented without any further delay. Compliance report shall be submitted. 4. At this juncture, Mr. Jawed Memon, Project Director, Sindh Flood Emergency Rehabilitation Project present contends that authorities have decided to deal all the issues relating to flood through this emergency project. In these circumstances, when admittedly whole irrigation system is compromised owing to present flood. It is therefore, pertinent to mention that project cannot be left without having no roadmap and planning in sight.

In these circumstances, when admittedly whole irrigation system is compromised owing to present flood. It is therefore, pertinent to mention that project cannot be left without having no roadmap and planning in sight. Hence master plan of drainage schemes and process of eight components as referred in para 15 of order dated 12.01.2023 shall be prepared and completed under rehabilitation damages funds. All encroachments on natural ways shall be removed immediately. Flood Commission Reports 2012/2013 shall be implemented in letter and spirit. However ongoing schemes under Sindh Flood Emergency Rehabilitation (FERP), Irrigation component shall be continued by all means of transparency under the guidelines and MoU with donor agencies/World Bank. 5. Besides, it has come on record that Sindh Flood Emergency Rehabilitation Program (SFERP) infrastructures, livelihoods and Rescue 1122 components have been established on 5th December 2022 in the Executive Committee of National Council of Common (ECNEC), hence Project Director (SFERP) shall be in attendance along with complete record of funds and their utilization on ongoing schemes. 6. Accordingly, Chief Secretary Sindh with consultation of Finance Department, Secretary Irrigation and others shall workout and submit feasible plan (master plan) to ensure that in upcoming years masses must be saved from this climate change flood(s). This C.P. No.D-1064 of 2022 & others 5 | P a g e exercise shall be completed within fifteen days with compliance report. 7. Director, PDMA present submits report showing therein that DG (PDMA) along with Secretaries of the relevant Departments conducted open Katcheries, during that directives have been issued to the Deputy Commissioners (DCs) as well that they shall conduct survey and submit their demand with regard to relief items. Accordingly, Deputy Commissioners of all four districts i.e.

Director, PDMA present submits report showing therein that DG (PDMA) along with Secretaries of the relevant Departments conducted open Katcheries, during that directives have been issued to the Deputy Commissioners (DCs) as well that they shall conduct survey and submit their demand with regard to relief items. Accordingly, Deputy Commissioners of all four districts i.e. Ghotki, Khairpur, Sukkur and Naushahro Feroze shall ensure that they have submitted demand after re-survey of the flood effected areas of their respective districts within three days; besides, DG PDMA shall ensure that all demands submitted are fulfilled and flood relief items shall be provided to the DCs within one week without fail. They shall also ensure that quality and standard of the flood relief items including blanket and warm clothes i.e. sweaters, jerseys and pullovers proposed to be provided to the flood effectees should be appropriate and reasonable for utilization and in this regard, PQ&SA shall monitor/observe the same diligently and carefully. DCs shall ensure distribution of the flood relief items in transparent manner, among the flood effectees with full respect and reverence, without any discrimination including Political affiliation and no one shall remain unattended; Whereas, homeless persons, who are known as High Risk Families having no permanent abode shall be provided all basic facilities. 8. Director PDMA contends that Provincial Disaster Management Authority provided 9068, 86200, 61000 and 34000 tents to the Districts, Ghotki, Khairpur, Naushahro Feroze and Sukkur, respectively. Controversially, Deputy Commissioners Khairpur and Sukkur, present in Court, categorically disputes the figures provided by Director, PDMA. According to Deputy Commissioner, Khairpur, he has received only 65000 tents, whereas Deputy Commissioner, Sukkur has received 18000 tents and similar position is reported in district Naushahro Feroze. Accordingly, DG (PDMA) shall be in attendance and explains his position with regard to tents as claimed

According to Deputy Commissioner, Khairpur, he has received only 65000 tents, whereas Deputy Commissioner, Sukkur has received 18000 tents and similar position is reported in district Naushahro Feroze. Accordingly, DG (PDMA) shall be in attendance and explains his position with regard to tents as claimed C.P. No.D-1064 of 2022 & others 6 | P a g e by Deputy Commissioners. In case of failure, contempt proceedings shall be initiated against him. 9. At this juncture, Director (PDMA) contends that in their survey conducted under the supervision of the Military Personnel, there is claim of 4000 casualties due to electric shocks, roof damages, drowning and snake bites and according to them, they have paid one million to the legal heirs of each deceased person up-today and they have settled the claim of 232 persons died in the present flood. Whereas, rest of the claims will be settled after seeking verification reports from Deputy Commissioners. Hence, the Deputy Commissioners shall conduct complete survey, with the help of NADRA & BISP and submit complete details along-with relevant documents like death certificate to PDMA within fifteen days. PDMA authorities are hereby directed to pay the compensation to the legal heirs of the victims within one month after proper verification from concern quarters and all the payment shall be made through Banking Channels in the account of the legal heirs; Moreover, appropriate compensation to those flood effectees be paid, who have lost their whole earnings in shape of dwellings owing to such flood in all districts of the Province of Sindh. As reported, some of flood affected areas have been surveyed; however, some are remained to be surveyed, thus prompt steps in this regard be taken to complete the entire survey immediately in order to compensate them accordingly from the funds reserved for flood effectees with availability of their data with NADRA & BISP. 10. It has come on record that Vehri Lake is occupied by private Respondents at the side of RD-40 Lath Waro Minor, which is a natural way of drainage and due such illegal occupation, the stagnant water could not be drained out.

As reported, some of flood affected areas have been surveyed; however, some are remained to be surveyed, thus prompt steps in this regard be taken to complete the entire survey immediately in order to compensate them accordingly from the funds reserved for flood effectees with availability of their data with NADRA & BISP. 10. It has come on record that Vehri Lake is occupied by private Respondents at the side of RD-40 Lath Waro Minor, which is a natural way of drainage and due such illegal occupation, the stagnant water could not be drained out. Accordingly, Deputy Commissioners shall form a committee comprising of concerned officers of SCARP, Director Agriculture, Representative of Abadgar Association, Representative of PW&D, Director, SIDA, who shall be competent to take action in accordance with law and ensure removal of encroachment(s), if any, over there. Moreover, with regard to C.P. No.D-1064 of 2022 & others 7 | P a g e stagnant water in Mirwah, Project Director, Khairpur shall provide Jhatta and ensure that stagnant water is drained from Mirwah. The Deputy Commissioners in all respective districts shall also ensure by directing the concerned officials to drain out the stagnant water at earliest under monitoring of Assistant Commissioners and shall submit their names in court, to held responsible in case any slackness is found on their part; Besides that, Executive Engineer, Highway, present contends that department has submitted PC for new road towards Mirwah. Accordingly, Works and Services Department shall look into the issue and ensure that such PC-(I or II) is approved and funds are provided. 11. At this juncture, it would be conducive to refer the judgment of Hon’ble Apex Court, wherein order of this Court has been modified to the extent of role of District Judiciary including Civil and District Judges, therefore, paragraph-24 of order dated 16.01.2023, passed by this Court, stands modified to that extent accordingly; Besides, Apex Court judgment contends that there must be role of women to look after committees, therefore, Deputy Commissioners shall ensure appropriate representation of women Officials, activists, non-government organizations and look after committees with regard to issues related to female genders.

Accordingly, Works and Services Department shall look into the issue and ensure that such PC-(I or II) is approved and funds are provided. 11. At this juncture, it would be conducive to refer the judgment of Hon’ble Apex Court, wherein order of this Court has been modified to the extent of role of District Judiciary including Civil and District Judges, therefore, paragraph-24 of order dated 16.01.2023, passed by this Court, stands modified to that extent accordingly; Besides, Apex Court judgment contends that there must be role of women to look after committees, therefore, Deputy Commissioners shall ensure appropriate representation of women Officials, activists, non-government organizations and look after committees with regard to issues related to female genders. Needless to add, the UN Women also plans to work in flood affected areas throughout Pakistan and has made appeal in this respect as well1 to provide life-saving relief including in Sindh & Balochistan provinces. It also includes provisional women’s centres and services for the prevention of and response to gender-based violence (GBV), with a focus on psychosocial support to GBV survivors2. Thusly, propriety demands that such kind of organization be taken on board to achieve desired relief results. Therefore, the Chief Secretary Sindh and Registrar of this Court shall take on board the UN Women Pakistan3 and submit response in this regard before the court. 1https://asiapacific.unwomen.org/en/countries/pakistan/flood-appeal 2https://pakistan.un.org/en/198187-un-women-puts-women-and-girls-heart-flood-response- pakistan 3https://asiapacific.unwomen.org/en/about-us/contact-us-4#pakistan C.P. No.D-1064 of 2022 & others 8 | P a g e 12. Beside that, Hon’ble Apex Court in the case of Marvi Memon Vs. Federation of Pakistan and others (PLD 2011 Supreme Court 854) constituted a flood commission and such petition was disposed of with the following orders:- 18. Thus, accordingly following directions are issued; (i). The Federal and Provincial Governments through Secretary Cabinet and Secretary Interior Division as well as Chief Secretaries of all the Provinces are hereby directed to implement the findings and recommendations of the report in letter and spirit. (ii). The report so prepared by the Commission shall be supplied, both soft and hard copies, to all concerned immediately. (iii). The Secretary Information of Federal and Provincial Governments are hereby directed to ensure publication of the findings and recommendations of the Commission and the instant order widely in print media as well as in electronic media in all the provinces and Islamabad Capital Territory in national and local languages. (iv).

The report so prepared by the Commission shall be supplied, both soft and hard copies, to all concerned immediately. (iii). The Secretary Information of Federal and Provincial Governments are hereby directed to ensure publication of the findings and recommendations of the Commission and the instant order widely in print media as well as in electronic media in all the provinces and Islamabad Capital Territory in national and local languages. (iv). The compliance report for our perusal in Chambers shall be sent fortnightly by the Chief Secretaries. [Emphasis added] 13. Nonetheless, despite repeated directions in last 05 months, PDMA has failed to provide complete relief to the flood affectees as well as they have failed to drain the flood water from respective areas. P.D SCARP Sukkur, Deputy Commissioner, Khairpur and representative of the Deputy Commissioners Ghotki, Sukkur and Naushahro Feroze have admitted that the thousands acres of the land is still submerged and IDPs are facing miseries of the flood water by taking shelter in different places. The reason assigned behind their contentions is non-availability of water ways to drain the flood water, but there is no explanation advanced regarding lack of steps being C.P. No.D-1064 of 2022 & others 9 | P a g e taken since promulgation of the Act, especially in respect of current disaster. Further they have contended that in initial period they received funds accordingly and the relief provided to the IDPs as well, but from one-month despite of making their requisition the concerned authorities have failed to provide them funds, therefore, they are unable to drain the water and provide relief to the IDPs. The above contentions of P.D SCARP Sukkur, Deputy Commissioner, Khairpur and representative of the Deputy Commissioners Ghotki, Sukkur and Naushahro Feroze depicts they have no co-ordination with the commissions and authorities and even they have not prepared the long term or short-term plans for extending relief(s) and shelter to the flood affectees as provided under the Act. It is an undeniable fact that the Government has allocated and released billions of rupees for providing relief and shelter to the flood affectees, nevertheless it is also an irrefutable and painful fact that these funds have not been incurred upon the affectees, in its letter and spirit and the elements of misappropriation, corruption and favourtism in disbursement of funds heard from every corner and cannot be ruled out. 14.

The above contentions of P.D SCARP Sukkur, Deputy Commissioner, Khairpur and representative of the Deputy Commissioners Ghotki, Sukkur and Naushahro Feroze depicts they have no co-ordination with the commissions and authorities and even they have not prepared the long term or short-term plans for extending relief(s) and shelter to the flood affectees as provided under the Act. It is an undeniable fact that the Government has allocated and released billions of rupees for providing relief and shelter to the flood affectees, nevertheless it is also an irrefutable and painful fact that these funds have not been incurred upon the affectees, in its letter and spirit and the elements of misappropriation, corruption and favourtism in disbursement of funds heard from every corner and cannot be ruled out. 14. It has also come on record that the IDPs are facing different miseries including various diseases Malaria, skin and bacterial infection; though there are serious allegations of misappropriation and discrimination with regard to the funds received by Disaster Management Committee, headed by the Deputy Commissioner(s). Be that as it may, Secretary Health and District Health Officers of District Khairpur Ghotki, Sukkur and Naushahro Feroze are directed to provide health facilities on emergent basis by establishing temporary dispensaries and providing mobile medical units (fully equipped with the necessary medicines, life savings drug, ASV and ARV vaccines coupled with medical and para-medical staff) in the relief camps till the IDPs C.P. No.D-1064 of 2022 & others 10 | P a g e go back to their homes. In case of any unpleasant incident with regard to the issues of health of the affectees, the Secretary Health and the District Health Officer of the concerned district will be held responsible for any unpleasant incident. 15. In view of above facts and circumstances so emerged; besides gross negligence and slackness on the part of different departments of Government of Sindh, which resulted in thousand casualties, damages to dwellings, crops and livestock, therefore, let show cause notices be issued to Secretary (Irrigation), Secretary (Revenue), Secretary (Works & Service Department) and DG PDMA, who shall be in attendance before this Court without fail and submit their explanations as to why “contempt proceedings” should not be initiated against them. Adjourned; to come up on 08.02.2023; to be taken up at 11:00 a.m., whereas, all officials present today shall remain in attendance along-with Deputy Commissioners, Naushahro Feroze and Sukkur without fail.

In view of above facts and circumstances so emerged; besides gross negligence and slackness on the part of different departments of Government of Sindh, which resulted in thousand casualties, damages to dwellings, crops and livestock, therefore, let show cause notices be issued to Secretary (Irrigation), Secretary (Revenue), Secretary (Works & Service Department) and DG PDMA, who shall be in attendance before this Court without fail and submit their explanations as to why “contempt proceedings” should not be initiated against them. Adjourned; to come up on 08.02.2023; to be taken up at 11:00 a.m., whereas, all officials present today shall remain in attendance along-with Deputy Commissioners, Naushahro Feroze and Sukkur without fail. Office to facsimile copy of this order to all concerned for strict compliance; besides copy be also provided to Additional Advocate General Office and Additional Attorney General, Registrar for information and compliance. Office is also directed to categorize all the captioned petitions district wise and listed them with separate headings in the cause list. JUDGE JUDGE Faisal Mumtaz/PS
